name: Marxer 2023 CZ (33 ns, IQM long-distance coupler)

provenance.citation: J. Marxer et al. (IQM), "Long-Distance Transmon Coupler with CZ-Gate Fidelity above 99.8%", PRX Quantum 4, 010314 (2023)
provenance.notes: Tunable floating-transmon-coupler CZ, 33 ns (22 ns interaction + 11 ns wait). Measured F_avg = (99.81 +/- 0.02)%, gate error eps_CZ = (1.9 +/- 0.2)e-3 by interleaved RB; authors state 'the gate is mostly coherence limited' with a computed coherence limit eps_limit = 1.7e-3 (Eq. 3 / Sec. IV). EVERY number below is quoted from the paper; the only computed quantity is each T2, which the harness derives in code via 1/T2 = 1/(2 T1) + 1/Tphi (gradpulse.literature.effective_t2_ns), so no hand-computed T2 sits in this file. WHY GATE-EFFECTIVE, NOT STATIC: the gate runs with the qubits parked while the COUPLER is flux-pulsed and hybridizes with them, so the qubits' coherence during the gate is the coupler-trajectory-weighted EFFECTIVE value the authors compute (T1,eff: Q1=14us, Q2=43us; exponential dephasing Tphi,1,eff: Q1=67us, Q2=43us), NOT the static idling-point Table III values (Q1 T1=13/T2=14, Q2 T1=42/T2=8 us). Feeding gradpulse the static values instead OVER-predicts the error by ~1.7x (analytic floor 3.2e-3 vs measured 1.9e-3) precisely because the static idling T2 is measured with the coupler un-pulsed; the effective times land at 1.0x. This static-vs-effective sensitivity is asserted in tests/test_literature.py, so the choice is auditable, not buried. GAUSSIAN DEPHASING: the authors also report a Gaussian dephasing component (Tphi,2,eff: Q1=17us, Q2=6us; quoted below as tphi_gauss_ns) contributing (tg/Tphi,2)^2 ~ 1.4e-5 (~0.8% of eps_limit). gradpulse's single-T2 Lindblad model is Markovian and cannot represent it; it is deliberately NOT folded into T2 (that would hide a real model limitation), so gradpulse's floor omits ~0.8% of the limit -- below GRAPE run-to-run scatter. Non-Markovian dephasing is the domain of quasi_static_fidelity / filter functions, not the coherence floor. Frequencies/anharmonicities at the two-qubit-gate idling point (Table III). g_max_mhz / omega_max_mhz are gradpulse parametric-model knobs (coupler dispersively eliminated), not device claims; the device qubit-coupler coupling is >50 MHz.
